concerns.text: Task T019 implements a generic Chi-squared test but fails to wire the Block Bootstrap logic (defined in Plan and T017/T020) required to generate valid p-values for dependent data. The Plan explicitly rejects standard asymptotic p-values for this sequence, making T019's deliverable a semantic weakening of FR-003 and Plan Methodology.

Task T020 describes comparing D_obs against a bootstrap distribution but lacks the explicit instruction to use the Block Bootstrap (T017) to generate that distribution. Without this explicit linkage, the task risks implementing a naive i.i.d. bootstrap or failing to generate the null distribution entirely, violating Constitution Principle VII (Empirical Statistical Rigor).

Task T012 specifies checking memory 'every 10^5 iterations' but omits the requirement to calculate the 90% threshold dynamically against the system's 7 GB limit as mandated by FR-007. This creates a hard-coded simplification that may fail to trigger the guard on systems with different RAM or miss the 90% trigger if the iteration count doesn't align with memory growth.

Task T027 requires annotating error bounds from Lebowitz-Lockard (2021) and Pollack & Roy (2024) but provides no mechanism to retrieve the specific formulas/constants. The Spec Assumptions note these are 'applicable as a reference' without defining the exact implementation details, creating a high risk that the task cannot be completed without unallocated research, violating the 'no silent constitution drift' principle.
